sutta: where the buddha talk about 4 kinds of person a per 1 person speaks in displays of what deserves not praise but does not speak in praise of what deserves praise then a second person he speaks in praise of what deserves praise but not in disgrace of what deserves not praise and then a third person he speaks neither in this praise of what deserves not praise nor in praise of the praiseworthy and the fourth type of person he speaks both in this place of what deserves not praise and in praise of what is praiseworthy and the buddha are somebody of these 4 types of person which is the best and then this person gave an answer but the buddha did not agree and then the buddha said the best person is 1 who speaks in disgrace of what deserves not praise and in praise of what is praiseworthy in other words what is what should be displaced he disgraces what should be praised he praises the buddha said that is the most admirable person because he talks very straight he discriminates on the proper occasion so uh we have to know there's a difference between being personal and telling facts [Music] now the nexus is
